Configurations, drivers, system utilities, and tests for Snowy platform boot process, including firmware updates and error handling.

## Folders
- **[board](board/README.md)**: Configurations and data structures for Snowy platform boards, including I2C, power, buttons, and peripherals.
- **[drivers](drivers/README.md)**: Drivers and headers for display, flash, PMIC, STM32 functions, and various peripheral interfaces.
- **[system](system/README.md)**: Functions and utilities for boot management, error handling, firmware storage, logging, and system resets.
- **[util](util/README.md)**: Macros, helper functions, encoding, CRC-32, delay, utility functions, network conversions, and version management.

## Files
- **[boot_tests.c](boot_tests.c.md)**: Tests for stuck buttons and flash memory integrity during boot.
- **[boot_tests.h](boot_tests.h.md)**: Declares functions to check if a button is stuck or if the flash is broken.
- **[firmware.h](firmware.h.md)**: Defines the firmware base address for the STM32F4 microcontroller platform.
- **[flash_region.h](flash_region.h.md)**: Defines memory layout and constants for flash regions in the Pebble firmware.
- **[fw_copy.c](fw_copy.c.md)**: Manages firmware updates, including validation, erasure, writing, and recovery processes.
- **[fw_copy.h](fw_copy.h.md)**: Functions for checking firmware updates and switching to recovery mode in case of failures.
- **[git_version.auto.h.in](git_version.auto.h.in.md)**: Header file for defining Git version information placeholders.
- **[hardfault_handler.c](hardfault_handler.c.md)**: Handles hard faults by logging the event and either entering a loop or performing a system reset.
- **[main.c](main.c.md)**: Bootloader for initializing hardware, checking system states, and transitioning to firmware execution.
- **[pebble_errors.h](pebble_errors.h.md)**: Defines error codes for firmware and Bluetooth issues in the Pebble firmware.
- **[stm32f_flash_boot.ld.in](stm32f_flash_boot.ld.in.md)**: Linker script for STM32F2xx with memory and section definitions for firmware boot process.
